content.problemStatement:
A vertex-induced subgraph of a simple graph GGG is one that can be obtained from GGG by removing some (possibly, none) vertices. When a vertex is removed, all edges incident to it must also be removed.

A vertex-induced cycle is a vertex-induced subgraph that is also a cycle.

Equivalently, a cycle CCC is vertex induced, if for every edge eee in the graph GGG that does not belong to CCC, **at most** one of its ends lies on CCC.

You are given a **simple** undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices and MMM edges. Each edge has a label that is either 000 or 111.

In one move, you can select any vertex-induced cycle in GGG and flip the values of the label in all edges of the cycle, (i.e. change every 000 to a 111 and every 111 to a 000).
The cost of one such move is equal to the size (i.e. number of edges) of the cycle.
The total cost for a sequence of moves is the sum of the costs of each individual move.

Determine if there exists a sequence of moves after which all edges have label 000. If such a sequence exists, then find a sequence of moves whose total cost does not exceed 3⋅k3 \cdot k3⋅k, where kkk is the number of edges in GGG that have label 111 **before any moves are done**; otherwise, print −1-1−1.

It's guaranteed that if an answer exists, there exists a sequence of moves with a total cost not exceeding 3⋅k3 \cdot k3⋅k.

**Note:** Test data contains large input files. It is recommended that you use fast input/output methods.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of the TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,MN, MN,M — the number of vertices and edges of the graph respectively.
The next MMM lines of each test case contain three space-separated integers u,vu, vu,v, and www, denoting an edge between vertices u,vu, vu,v with label www.


Output Format

For each test case, print the answer starting from a new line in the following format:
If it is impossible to convert all edge labels to 000 as per the statement, then print −1-1−1.
Otherwise, in the first line print the number of operations that you require to perform, (say xxx).
In the iii-th of the next xxx lines, print the number of vertices of the cycle you choose in the iii-th move followed by the vertices of the cycle. The vertices must be printed in either **clockwise or anticlockwise order**. That is, print s x1 x2 … xss\ x_1\ x_2\ \ldots\ x_ss x1​ x2​ … xs​ to denote a cycle of size sss whose edges are (x1,x2),(x2,x3),…,(xs−1,xs),(xs,x1)(x_1, x_2), (x_2, x_3), \ldots, (x_{s-1}, x_s), (x_s, x_1)(x1​,x2​),(x2​,x3​),…,(xs−1​,xs​),(xs​,x1​).
If there are multiple correct outputs, you may print **any** of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000
The sum of kkk over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000
The given graph is a simple graph, i.e. there are no self loops or multiple edges

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:
In this example N=6,M=9N=6, M=9N=6,M=9 and k=9k=9k=9. The total cost of the sequence of moves given is 151515.
The graph and the sequence of moves are shown below.

Note that any solution for which the total cost is no more than 3⋅k=273 \cdot k = 273⋅k=27 is also considered correct.
For example another solution is shown below with total cost equal to 999.

Test Case 2: The graph is same as the previous testcase, except that the edge labels are different (k=6k = 6k=6). After the first move, the resulting graph is identical to the first case.
The total cost is 121212 which is less than 3⋅k=183 \cdot k = 183⋅k=18.
The solution for this case is shown below.

Note that the cycle formed by vertices 1,2,3,4,5,61, 2, 3, 4, 5, 61,2,3,4,5,6 is not a vertex induced cycle.
Test Case 3: The graph does not have any edge with label 111, so no operations are required.
Test Case 4: The graph does not contain any cycles, and therefore it is impossible to flip the label of any edge (i.e. no valid move exists).
